Electronic Press Kit  ·  2026

BintiAfrika

Swahili · Dub · Queen
Listen Now Book Her

Binti Afrika is East Africa's pioneering Swahili Dub artist — the first to build a sound where conscious Kiswahili lyricism rides heavyweight roots reggae and the raw energy of sound system culture.

Over two decades at the heart of East Africa's reggae scene, she has headlined festivals, shared stages with international icons, and carried the sound to global audiences. She is the founder of Kenyan Reggae Magazine and Kenyan Reggae Night — not just one of the scene's most powerful voices, but one of the people actively building it.

As champion of the Lioness Order — a movement of sisterhood, strength and the upliftment of female youth — she carries both personal conviction and collective purpose.

20+ Years Active
50K+ Streams
3 Reggaeville Premieres
#TheLionessOrder
"Our Culture. Our Sound. Our Power."
The Sound · The Fire · The Movement
She carries the fire.
Always has.
Twenty years of Swahili Dub. Heavyweight roots. Conscious Kiswahili lyricism built for global stages — and for the streets she came from.
Featured Releases

Essential
Catalogue

2026
★ Landmark Release
Skidli Bang
ft. Cienfuego & Nazizi
Selecta Gimme Dat Riddim · First All-African Women Reggae Riddim

The most significant release of 2026. Places Binti alongside Nazizi on the first all-African women reggae riddim — a landmark for the Lioness Order: sisterhood, strength and African female artistry demanding its place on the global stage.

SpotifyApple MusicAmazonTidalDeezer
2024
50,000+ Streams
Tusker na Reggae
ft. Shamir Tadeiya · Austad Sound, Norway
Executive Produced in Oslo · Breakthrough International Single

A Nairobi–Oslo collaboration that crossed 50,000 streams and introduced Swahili Dub to European reggae audiences. Built for festival sound systems, this is the track that opened the international door.

SpotifyApple MusicTidalDeezerMdundoBoomplay
2025
In This Together
Austad Sound · Lucy Lugano · Shamir Tadeiya
Anthemic Roots-Reggae · European & East African Stages

Three East African voices under Norwegian production. One of the most internationally oriented tracks in her catalogue — built for stages that bridge African and global reggae audiences.

SpotifyApple MusicYouTube
2021
Reggaeville Premiere
Conky Fyah
ft. Hopetone Blazze
Prayer Riddim · Ever Blazing Productions

An official video premiere on Europe's leading reggae platform. The track that put Binti Afrika's name firmly on the global reggae map — raw, roots, and unmistakably Swahili Dub.

ReggaevilleSpotifyApple MusicYouTube
2023
Still a Roar
ft. Checkmate Mido · Ras Nelson · Milz Music
Pan-African · Kenya · Cape Verde · Zambia

The track that best captures Binti's role as a connector within the African reggae diaspora. Conscious lyricism, cross-continental collaboration, defiant African resistance.

SpotifyApple MusicYouTube
2019
Reggaeville Premiere
Barua Kwa Serikali
Solo
Doctor Darling Riddim · Political Commentary

A defining political statement in Kiswahili that earned an official Reggaeville premiere and introduced Binti Afrika to the global reggae conversation. Direct, powerful, unapologetic.

Nairobi, Kenya · The World
Live Performance

On
Stage

Kiswahili vocals over heavyweight dub with conscious, identity-rooted lyrics. A commanding stage presence that moves from intimate sessions to festival crowds, carrying the sound to global audiences from the first riddim.

Genre: Reggae / Swahili Dub / Afro-Fusion  ·  Setup: Fully Scalable

Format 01 · Intimate
Vocals + Selector

Vocals with selector/DJ and minimal dub backing. Suited to clubs, showcases, cultural venues and seated festival stages.

Format 02 · Mid
Vocals + Percussion

Vocals + DJ/selector + percussion with live dub effects. The standard touring configuration — full energy, flexible footprint.

Format 03 · Festival
Full Sound System

Full live band + sound system, expandable with percussion, horns and backing vocals. Built for main stages and major festivals globally.

Documentary
UndergroundExpressions
Women in Music & Politics in Kenya

Binti Afrika is a featured artist in Underground Expressions, a documentary series on Kenyan women musicians whose work speaks directly to political and social realities — a vital institutional record of her artistic mission.

Music & Politics Women's Voices Swahili Dub Cultural Identity
Premiere
November 25, 2021 · Nairobi, Kenya

Book
Binti Afrika

Available for international festivals, cultural programmes, headline shows and sound system events. Scalable setup from intimate to full festival production. Based in Nairobi — available for international travel.
